item.title: SVM01 - HyperPlane, Support Vectors and Maximal Margin Classification
item.author: Author
item.description: SVM 1. Maximal Margin Classifier 1.1. What is a HyperPlane? Definition: In a p-dimensional space, a HyperPlane is a flat affine subspace of dimension p-1 affine: the subspace need not pass through the origin if $p=2$, a hyperplane is a flat 1-dimensional subspace in other words, a Line => $\beta_0 + \beta_1X_1 + \beta_2X_2 = 0$ if $p=3$, a hyperplane is a flat 2-dimensional subspace in other words, a Plane If $p>3$: the p-dimensional hyperlane is defined by the equation: $$\beta_0 + \beta_1X_1 + \beta_2X_2 + … + \beta_pX_p= 0$$
